title: Conversation
summary: Connect your sentences: but, and, or, if — and keep a conversation going.

teach.note: Same spelling as 'yes' — context makes it clear, like 'si' in Spanish/Italian.

teach.target: Io parla interlingua, ma io ancora apprende.
teach.roman: io parla interlingua ma io ancora apprende
teach.english: I speak Interlingua, but I'm still learning.

exercises.ttsText: io parla interlingua ma io ancora apprende
choices: Io parla interlingua, ma io ancora apprende.

exercises.prompt: Fill the blank: "___ tu vole, nos vade al parco." (If you want, we'll go to the park)

teach.note: non before the verb negates it.
poter = to be able; pote = can.

teach.english: How do you say ... in Interlingua?

teach.note: Adverbs end in -mente, like Romance languages.

grammar.title: Negation and adverbs
grammar.body:
Negate any verb with **non** before it: io comprende → io **non** comprende.
Make adverbs with **-mente** on the adjective: natural → natural**mente** (naturally), felice → felice**mente** (happily).
With connectors, questions, three tenses, and negation, you can now improvise real conversations — the rest is vocabulary.
